disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden worsening of central vision in one eye over 48 hours in someone with long-standing slowly-progressive central vision loss
trigger_question_examples: What has changed with your vision, and how quickly?
Is this different from your usual eyesight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My central vision in the right eye has suddenly got much worse over the last two days — it's always been slowly declining but nothing this fast before.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Straight lines appear bent and wavy (distortion) with a dark patch in the centre of vision
trigger_question_examples: Do straight lines look distorted or wavy?
Is there a blank or dark spot in your vision?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the door frame looks bent and wavy and there's a dark smudge right in the middle when I look straight at things.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Peripheral vision fully preserved — can navigate the room without bumping into things
trigger_question_examples: Can you still see out of the sides of that eye?
Is any part of your vision still normal?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The sides are fine — I can see around the edges and get about the room, it's just the middle that's gone.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No eye pain, no headache, no nausea, no coloured haloes around lights
trigger_question_examples: Is the eye painful at all?
Any headache, nausea, or haloes around lights?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, there's no pain in the eye and no headache or sickness, and no rings around lights — it just doesn't hurt.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden shower of new floaters, no flashing lights, and no curtain or shadow coming down over the vision
trigger_question_examples: Have you seen any new floaters or flashing lights?
Does it feel like a curtain or shadow is coming across your sight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No flashes and no new floaters, and nothing like a curtain coming down — it's just the distortion and the dark spot in the middle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing slowly-progressive central vision loss labelled 'dry' macular trouble years ago
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any known eye conditions?
Have you been told anything about your eyes before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A couple of years ago I was told I had the 'dry' kind of macular degeneration and my central vision has slowly been getting worse since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone, high anxiety, and unsteadiness / fear of falling from the sudden loss of central depth perception
trigger_question_examples: How are you managing at home with this?
Are you worried about getting around safely?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and I'm frightened — I feel off-balance with the vision like this and I'm scared of falling.
